'''Caleb Aekins''' (born 21 November 1997) is a [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] international professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Canberra Raiders]] and [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] and for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[English Super League]].

==Background==
Aekins was born in [[Hamilton, New Zealand]].

He played both [[rugby union]] and rugby league as a junior for the Otaika Eagles and Hikurangi Stags in rugby league and captained Whangarei Boys High School, in 2015 he moved over to Australia. Aekins is of [[Māori people|Māori]] and Welsh descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.walesrugbyleague.co.uk/article/16163/meet-the-team-caleb-aekins |title=Meet the team: Caleb Aekins |publisher=Wales Rugby League |date=17 August 2017}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Penrith Panthers ===
Aekins made his [[NRL]] debut in round 18 of the [[2018 NRL season]] at fullback for Penrith against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/5518661/pearce-to-return-for-knights-in-nrl/ |title=Pearce to return for Knights in NRL |publisher=Newcastle Herald |date=10 July 2018}}</ref>

Aekins made eight appearances for Penrith in the [[2020 NRL season]] as the club won the [[Minor Premiership]].  He did not play in the 2020 finals series or Penrith's [[2020 NRL Grand Final]] loss to Melbourne.

===Canberra Raiders ===
He was then released by Penrith and signed a contract to join Canberra for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2020-transfer-centre-contract-news-offcontract-players-melbourne-storm/news-story/a1d403f5fbebe2b170174ad5103d6665?fbclid=IwAR0SizH39Jk9vhUjIx6zeByhYPFrej0fPcUJaMKDooT4ncZnh7DGzU_5LNc/|title=Penrith confirm departure of six players|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=30 October 2020}}</ref>

In round 6 of the [[2021 NRL season]], he made his debut for Canberra in their 35–10 loss against [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-04-17/nrl-eels-canberra-rabbitohs-wests-tigers-sea-eagles-titans/100072470/|title=Parramatta beats Canberra 35-10 as South Sydney, Manly post contrasting NRL wins|newspaper=ABC News|date=17 April 2021}}</ref>
Aekins made seven appearances for Canberra in the [[2021 NRL season]] as the club finished 10th and missed the finals.

===Leigh Centurions===
Following the end of the season, Aekins was released by the Canberra side<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/01/23/2021-signings-tracker-official-rosters-and-contracts-for-all-16-clubs2/#raiders/|title=Peachey bound for Tigertown; Suli a Dragon|website=www.nrl.com|date=29 October 2021}}</ref> and on 10 November 2021 signed for British [[RFL Championship]] side [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] for the 2022 season.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-11-10|title=Leigh Centurions make statement with NRL fullback signing|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/leigh-centurions-make-statement-with-nrl-fullback-signing-45287/|access-date=2021-11-10|website=Serious About Rugby League|language=en-GB}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, Aekins played for Leigh in their [[2022 RFL 1895 Cup]] final victory over [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-1895-cup-final-defeat-to-leigh-centurions-gives-brian-mcdermotts-side-signal-of-their-progress-3713313/|title=Featherstone Rovers: 1895 Cup final defeat to Leigh Centurions gives Brian McDermott's side signal of their progress|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>
On 3 October 2022, Aekins played for Leigh in their [[Million Pound Game]] victory over [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] which saw the club promoted back to the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12710793/leigh-44-12-batley-centurions-seal-super-league-return-with-deserved-win-in-championship-grand-final/|title=Leigh 44-12 Batley: Centurions seal Super League return with deserved win in Championship Grand Final|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

===Featherstone===
On 29 November 2022, Aekins signed a two-year deal to join RFL Championship side Featherstone.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.featherstonerovers.co.uk/news/aekins-boosts-rovers-ranks-lc4mV/|title=Aekins boosts Rovers' ranks|website=www.featherstonerovers.co.uk}}</ref> On 5 November 2024, it was announced that Aekins had signed a two-year extension with the Rovers.<ref>{{Cite web |author=Staff Writer |date=2024-11-05 |title=Ex-Leigh Leopards, NRL ace secures Championship future with length of contract revealed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ex-leigh-leopards-nrl-ace-secures-championship-future-with-length-of-contract-revealed |access-date=2024-11-10 |website=Love Rugby League |language=en}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
On 10 November 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]] on a 1-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 Nov 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=Bradford Bulls swoop for overseas Championship stalwart with length of contract revealed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bradford-bulls-swoop-for-overseas-championship-stalwart-with-length-of-contract-revealed}}</ref>

==International career==
Aekins was named in the [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] squad for the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Wales Men’s Squad Named For World Cup |url=https://wrl.wales/wales-mens-squad-named-for-world-cup |publisher=Wales Rugby League |access-date=28 October 2022 |date=3 October 2022}}</ref> On 19 October 2022, he made his international debut for Wales in their 12–18 loss against the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] in the opening round of the group stages.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63318170|title=Rugby League World Cup: Wales 12-18 Cook Islands - Steve Marsters seals Kukis' victory|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
